title: Red Flag Detection in Financial Statements
tagline: Spot creative accounting and earnings manipulation in suspicious financials.
scenario: A company's revenue has grown 30% per year for 3 years, but operating cash flow has been flat. Accounts receivable has doubled. The auditors signed off, but something does not smell right.
brief: Analyse the hypothetical financial statements and identify the red flags. Explain the manipulation techniques that could produce these patterns and design 3 forensic checks an auditor should have performed.
deliverables: A list of at least 5 red flags with specific ratios or trends that support each
An explanation of the most likely manipulation technique (channel stuffing, premature revenue recognition, etc.)
Three forensic checks with the specific data or documents you would request
A one-paragraph opinion on whether these financials should be trusted
submissionGuidance: Revenue growing while cash flow stagnates is the classic red flag. But what specifically could cause it? Be precise about the mechanism.

rubric.label: Red Flag Identification
rubric.description: Are the red flags specific and supported by ratio analysis?

rubric.label: Manipulation Mechanism
rubric.description: Is the proposed manipulation technique credible and specific?

rubric.label: Forensic Checks
rubric.description: Would the proposed checks actually uncover the manipulation?

rubric.label: Professional Judgment
rubric.description: Is the opinion appropriately cautious and well-supported?
